intro.uz: Sogʻlom yurak — bu faqat parhez emas. John Robbins uzoq umr koʻrgan insonlarni oʻrganib, sevgi, gʻamxoʻrlik va odamlar bilan aloqa yurak uchun ovqat va sport kabi muhim ekanini topdi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi: qancha vaqtdan beri davom etayotgan odat (present perfect continuous — for / since / How long) va birovga qildirish (kausativ — have / get something done). Zach haqidagi POV hikoyada present perfect continuous eshitasiz; kausativni esa darsning oʻz misollarida mashq qilasiz.
intro.en: A healthy heart is not only about diet. Studying people who lived long, healthy lives, John Robbins found that love, caring and connection matter as much as food and exercise. This week's two grammar topics: how long a habit has been going on (present perfect continuous — for / since / How long) and arranging a service (causative — have / get something done). You'll hear the present perfect continuous in the POV story about Zach; the causative you'll practise through the lesson's own examples.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida “How long have you been …?” savoliga toʻxtamasdan javob bera olasiz va sogʻligʻingiz uchun qanday xizmatlarni qildirganingizni (get your heart checked) tabiiy ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can answer “How long have you been …?” out loud without pausing, and say what services you have done for your health (get your heart checked) naturally.

grammar.titleUz: for / since / How long + present perfect continuous
grammar.titleEn: Present perfect continuous — for / since / How long
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — say how long you have been doing a healthy habit
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can say how long I have been doing an activity (have/has been + -ing)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida buni oddiy hozirgi zamonda aytamiz: “Olti oydan beri yuguraman”. Ingliz tili oʻtmishda boshlanib hozirgacha davom etayotgan jarayon uchun present perfect continuous (have/has been + V-ing) ishlatadi. Bizning “beri” inglizchada ikkiga boʻlinadi: “since” (boshlanish nuqtasi) va “for” (davomiylik).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I am exercising since January. I study English for two years. ✅ I have been exercising since January. I have been studying English for two years. since/for bilan davom etayotgan jarayon kelsa — have/has been + -ing (oddiy hozirgi zamon emas).

examples.en: I have been exercising for six months.
examples.uz: Olti oydan beri mashq qilib kelyapman.

examples.en: She has been caring for her parents since 2019.
examples.uz: U 2019-yildan beri ota-onasiga gʻamxoʻrlik qilib kelmoqda.

examples.en: How long have you been walking every day?
examples.uz: Qancha vaqtdan beri har kuni yurasiz?

exercises.prompt: I ___ English every morning for a year now.
exercises.hintUz: for a year + hozirgacha davom etayotgan jarayon → have been + -ing

exercises.prompt: How long ___ you been walking every day?
exercises.hintUz: How long + present perfect continuous → have/has been + -ing
January = boshlanish nuqtasi → since; “for” esa davomiylik uchun

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “I have been … for/since … .” — masalan sogʻlom odatingiz haqida.

grammar.titleUz: Kausativ: have / get something done (birovga qildirish)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — talk about health services you arrange (checks, tests)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can say I arranged for a service to be done (have/get + object + past participle)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida “qildirish” maʼnosini feʼlga -tir/-dir qoʻshimchasi beradi (tekshir → tekshirtirmoq). Ingliz tilida alohida qoʻshimcha yoʻq — buni have/get + narsa + V3 qolipi bajaradi. Shuning uchun “I cut my hair” (oʻzim kesdim) va “I had my hair cut” (sartarosh kesdi) — butunlay boshqa maʼno.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I must check my heart. I had cut my hair. ✅ I must get my heart checked. I had my hair cut. Ishni boshqa birov bajarsa → have/get + narsa + V3 (feʼlning uchinchi shakli).

examples.en: I get my heart checked every year.
He had his blood tested at the clinic.
You should have your teeth cleaned twice a year.
examples.uz: Yiliga ikki marta tishlaringizni tozalatishingiz kerak.

exercises.prompt: I need to ___ my heart checked.
exercises.hintUz: kausativ qolip: get/have + narsa + V3 (get ... checked)

exercises.prompt: He had his blood ___ at the clinic.
You should get your teeth ___ twice a year.
exercises.hintUz: get + narsa + V3: cleaned (ishni tish shifokori bajaradi)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: “Last year I had/got … done.” — sogʻligʻingiz uchun qildirgan biror xizmatni ayting.
exercises.hintUz: have/get + narsa + V3 (checked, tested, cleaned, done)

vocab.defEn: long thin strings, like the threads in cloth
vocab.example: A thousand fibers connect us with our fellow men.

vocab.defEn: to feel strong emotion because of something
vocab.example: Zach was moved by the homeless man's suffering.

vocab.defEn: a consequence or result of something
vocab.example: There are medical implications to thinking only of yourself.
Dr. Scherwitz taped the conversations of 600 men.

vocab.defEn: very clever or smart, usually about an idea or method
vocab.example: Counting the times a person said 'I' was an ingenious way to measure selfishness.

vocab.uz: faqat oʻzi bilan band, oʻziga berilgan
vocab.defEn: thinking only about yourself all the time

vocab.example: Caring about other people is sound medical advice.

vocab.defEn: very easy to see; standing out
vocab.example: He was always very conspicuous on the street.
In sports people can become hyper-competitive.

vocab.defEn: to lose your connection with someone or something
vocab.example: He realized he had lost touch with his happiness.

englishpod.title: The Office — I need an assistant!

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda charchagan xodim boshligʻidan yordamchi soʻraydi — yolgʻiz koʻtargan yuki juda ogʻir. “Healthy Heart” darsi ham shuni oʻrgatadi: yolgʻiz boʻlmaslik, yukni boʻlishish yurak uchun foydali. Sizningcha, boshliq unga yordamchi topib beradimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a tired worker asks his boss for help — the weight he carries alone is too heavy. The Healthy Heart lesson teaches the same thing: not being alone and sharing the load is good for the heart. Do you think the boss will find him an assistant?

keyVocab.defEn: the money, people or materials available to do something
not having enough people to do the work

keyVocab.en: the timing is just not right
keyVocab.defEn: it is not a good time to do something now

keyVocab.en: take the weight off my shoulders
keyVocab.defEn: to remove some of the pressure or stress from someone

sixmin.title: Have you walked off your pizza?

quiz.q: How long would you have to walk to burn off the calories in a quarter of a large pizza?
quiz.qUz: Katta pitsaning choragidagi kaloriyani yoqish uchun qancha vaqt yurish kerak?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 83 minutes. Neil a) 33 minutes dedi va kam baholab xato qildi. Royal Society for Public Health maʼlumotiga koʻra, katta pitsaning atigi choragidagi kaloriyani yoqish uchun 83 daqiqa yurish kerak.

vocab.defEn: to use up energy or calories through activity
based on an understanding of the facts
to think carefully about doing something before you do it

funEnglish.title: Your Heart is a Muscle... This is How It Adapts to Exercise

speakingPrompt.en: How do you look after your heart? How long have you been building your healthy habits — and what checks or services do you get done to stay healthy?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: present perfect continuous (I have been … for/since …; How long have you been …?) va kausativ (I get my heart checked; I had my blood tested).
